notes: This hub is the source of truth for the public browser editorial UI pass.
Browser / becomes the public editorial gateway; installed PWA / still routes to /home.
/landing becomes a legacy redirect to /.
Homepage closing should be Get In Touch with POST /public/subscribe email subscription and Google appointment scheduling.
Funding UX is Donate or Endow first, then Card or Wallet where proven.
Reown AppKit remains the wallet funding baseline; thirdweb Pay is the preferred direct-card funding spike.
FundingIntent is a strict durable ledger with idempotent webhook reconciliation, explicit status transitions, quoted/minimum destination amount fields, and onchain-confirmed success.
FundingIntent creation is card-only for this pass; wallet funding remains Reown AppKit plus wagmi/viem without Agent receipt tracking.
Public funding receipt/status reads require an opaque receipt token and return only redacted public-safe receipt fields.
Receipt URLs use /fund?intent=<id>#receiptToken=<token>; clients send the token to the API only through X-GG-Receipt-Token.

Receipt-token analytics protection uses a dual guard: root/equivalent pre-pageview fragment scrub with history.replaceState before initial tracking, plus generic usePageView hash redaction for receiptToken and other sensitive hash keys.
Receipt-bearing funding create/read responses must set Cache-Control: no-store and Pragma: no-cache.

Public funding intent creation responses expose only allowlisted JSON-safe checkout payload data; durable provider ids, raw provider/webhook payloads, receipt tokens, and unknown nested checkout fields stay server-only and are redacted or rejected from public responses.
Card funding success is confirmed by Agent-side viem receipt/log polling; indexer evidence is secondary for v1.
Agent viem confirmation RPC resolves from existing chain-specific envs before shared network fallback, then injects that RPC URL into initBlockchain and the confirmation client.
Funding intent creation never subscribes a visitor to updates; receipt update signup calls POST /public/subscribe separately.
FundingIntent, thirdweb webhook/status, and Luma subscription work belong in packages/agent under the state_api lane.

Card FundingIntent creation includes destinationAddress, canonical v1 availabilityKey, and clientRequestId; Agent recomputes the exact live rail tuple, uses a pinned normalized idempotency fingerprint, deduplicates exact retries, and rejects mismatches, non-live card rails, or idempotency conflicts safely.
Agent API route classes are split into public rate-limited routes, provider webhooks, and bearer-protected internal/admin routes.

Public route rate-limit keys are route-specific and use a trusted-proxy policy with moderate v1 thresholds: subscribe 5/hour, funding create 10/10 minutes, receipt read 60/10 minutes, and thirdweb webhook pre-verification plus post-verification buckets at 300/minute each.
Browser-facing Agent routes are POST /public/subscribe, POST /public/funding-intents, and GET /public/funding-intents/:id; thirdweb callbacks use POST /webhooks/thirdweb.

Server-safe public contracts live under @green-goods/shared/public-contracts with package exports and client TypeScript/Vite resolution wired, and Agent imports must not pull the shared root or browser-only modules.
state_api migrates the packages/agent HTTP server from Fastify to Hono before adding new public subscription, funding-intent, or thirdweb webhook routes.
Hono migration must preserve existing health, ready, Telegram webhook, platform allowlist, /api/feedback, /api/notify, startup, shutdown, and /api/* bearer-auth behavior.
Public browser calls use existing VITE_API_BASE_URL; do not add VITE_AGENT_PUBLIC_API_URL unless a later deployment decision requires it.
Card Endow uses thirdweb embedded wallet only inside the card Endow path.
PublicFundingAvailability uses shared public reads where possible, returns explicit reasonCode/params rather than localized copy, and hides unproven card methods by default.

PublicFundingAvailability reasonCode excludes amount min/max failures; amount_below_min and amount_above_max are PublicApiErrorCode values returned only from selected-amount UI or server quote/create validation.
Provider proof registry is code-owned, exact-tuple keyed, hidden by default, and consumed by PublicFundingAvailability.
FundingIntent storage uses Agent SQLite patterns with migration/versioning, funding_intents, and funding_intent_events or equivalent history storage.
Cookie Jar Donate and Vault Endow reconciliation are destination-specific and provider success alone never marks an intent funded.
Card Endow completion shows receipt/recovery and routes management to Install/Open App.
Endow copy uses designed-to-preserve language with plain risk copy.
Donate/support copy must not imply tax deductibility, charitable status, nonprofit status, or legal receipt unless separately configured and reviewed.
FundingIntent includes storage/reconciliation guards, provider lookup keys, canonical availabilityKey, clientRequestId, transactionAttempts, fundingTxHash, and thirdweb purchase/custom data.

Abandoned card intents in started or pending_provider expire at provider checkout expiry, or createdAt plus 30 minutes when provider expiry is missing, through read-time reconciliation and an Agent scheduled sweep.
Late matching onchain success after expiry can move expired to funded_late only when intent/provider session and Garden/destination/receiver/token/chain/min amount all match.
Public Agent API and env contracts include origin controls, payload caps, consent audit, safe errors, and PII handling.
Luma signup is single opt-in with explicit consent.

/fund?garden resolves exact Garden id/address first and accepts slug only when exactly one value derived by publicGardenHelpers.deriveSlug matches; stale, missing, zero-match, or ambiguous references fall back to normal Fund with a localized non-blocking message.

/impact v1 uses shared EAS reads with deterministic latest-activity Garden slicing, page size 12, max 50 Gardens, max 100 assessment/evidence records, explicit per-Garden overfetch behavior, partialData/sourceLimitReached states, and EAS failure states before UI implementation.
UI may start once the contract_stability_checklist is complete; card rails that are not live stay hidden.
Public browser components should live in packages/client unless there is proven shared reuse.
No public-side conviction voting, public withdrawals, or new route families beyond browser /.

contract_stability_checklist.ui_unblocks_when: All required items are complete; UI does not wait for every card payment method to be live.
contract_stability_checklist.unblock_operation: After all required checklist items are complete, run: node scripts/harness/plan-hub.mjs set-lane --feature public-read-side-journal --lane ui --status ready --actor codex --note "contract stability checklist complete"

items.description: Concrete /public/* and /webhooks/thirdweb route paths are stable.

Framework-free public route/type contracts are exported from server-safe @green-goods/shared/public-contracts through packages/shared/package.json exports with client TypeScript/Vite resolution where needed, including the canonical availabilityKey builder, FundingTransactionAttempt types, explicit amount validation error codes, and allowlisted checkout payload types; public funding-intent create responses expose only allowlisted client-safe checkout data, and seeded handoff fixtures exist under artifacts/fixtures for funding availability, funding intents, contact subscription, /fund?garden, and /impact.

/fund?garden resolves exact id/address first, accepts only unique publicGardenHelpers.deriveSlug matches, and stale/missing/zero-match/ambiguous values fall back with a localized non-blocking message.

PublicFundingAvailability reasonCode values define allowed states, required params, UI localization expectations, and exclude selected-amount min/max failures; amount_below_min and amount_above_max are PublicApiErrorCode values only.
Subscribe endpoint validates email/consent through POST /public/subscribe and returns honest subscribed, already_subscribed, and failure states.

Funding receipt reads use fragment-to-X-GG-Receipt-Token transport, require root/equivalent pre-pageview fragment scrub plus usePageView hash redaction before analytics, return only public-safe redacted receipt fields, and set no-store/no-cache response headers.

Code-owned provider proof registry exists with exact tuple keys, hidden-by-default absence behavior, proof references for live card rails, canonical availabilityKey semantics for funding-intent drift detection, and compatibility with transaction-attempt funding proof.
/impact hook shape, deterministic Garden source ordering, local slicing limits, explicit per-Garden overfetch behavior, partialData/sourceLimitReached flags, and EAS failure states are stable.

ui.handoff_note: Implement public editorial homepage, route layouts, public card/dialog polish, install CTA, Get In Touch closing through POST /public/subscribe plus Google appointment link, Donate/Endow support-only funding choice, reasonCode-localized availability copy, amount validation separate from base availability with amount_below_min/amount_above_max PublicApiErrorCode handling, /fund?garden exact-id and publicGardenHelpers.deriveSlug unique-slug fallback messaging, card funding create destinationAddress/canonical availabilityKey/clientRequestId handling, allowlisted checkout payload consumption only, root pre-pageview receipt scrub plus usePageView hash redaction, receipt fundingTxHash display, hidden-by-default card states, Card Endow receipt/recovery/app handoff, risk-safe Endow copy, Donate/support tax/charity copy guard, accessible dialogs, unique/fallback motion behavior, and packages/client/DESIGN.browser.md update after contract_stability_checklist is complete.

checkout payload types, explicit amount validation error codes, and seeded fixtures under artifacts/fixtures, define fragment-to-header receipt-token protected public funding reads with root pre-pageview scrub, usePageView hash redaction, and no-store/no-cache response headers, return only allowlisted client-safe checkout data from public funding-intent creation, keep FundingIntent creation card-only and subscription-free, require destinationAddress, canonical availabilityKey, and clientRequestId with Agent recomputation/idempotency against live reads/proof registry using the pinned normalized fingerprint fields, define shared-read PublicFundingAvailability with explicit reasonCode state/params semantics, amount validation split, and code-owned provider proof registry, strict FundingIntent SQLite storage/reconciliation with transactionAttempts, canonical fundingTxHash, Agent viem receipt/log polling, server RPC resolver, minAssetAmount matching, abandoned contracts.handoff_note: No contract work expected for this public browser UI pass.

qa_pass_1.handoff_note: Run visual/browser/story validation after UI lane lands.

qa_pass_2.handoff_note: Run final repo gates and plan/design consistency checks after visual QA completes, including Hono compatibility proof, concrete public route paths, VITE_API_BASE_URL env alignment, server-safe public-contracts exports/resolution, canonical availabilityKey builder, allowlisted checkout payload proof, dual receipt-token analytics guards, no-store/no-cache receipt headers, destinationAddress/availabilityKey/clientRequestId funding create drift and pinned idempotency fingerprint checks, transactionAttempts/fundingTxHash sequencing, abandoned intent expiry plus funded_late late confirmation handling, moderate public route rate-limit thresholds with two-stage webhook throttling, explicit reasonCode enum/localization, amount validation error-code alignment, SQLite funding intent storage, destination-specific reconciliation, Donate/support copy guard, publicGardenHelpers.deriveSlug-backed /fund?garden fallback, UI unblock criteria, and /impact shared-EAS slicing limits.

history.note: Initial public browser read-side plan created.
Updated plan to locked editorial browser direction: / homepage gateway, /landing redirect, install CTA, page-by-page layout contracts, and lane handoffs. No runtime UI files changed.
Adjusted / homepage closing to Get In Touch with email subscription and Google appointment scheduling. No runtime UI files changed.
Added direct-card funding decision: keep Reown AppKit for wallet funding, spike thirdweb Pay for direct card funding, and use provider-neutral FundingIntent tracking. No runtime UI files changed.

Remediated plan review gaps: UI blocked on state_api, funding UX changed to Donate/Endow before Card/Wallet, FundingIntent and Luma subscription assigned to packages/agent, and direct-card success requires onchain confirmation. No runtime UI files changed.

Closed remaining plan gaps: split Agent API routes, strict FundingIntent ledger, card Endow embedded-wallet receiver/recovery, Luma single opt-in, public assessment/evidence hook, support-only funding language, and seeded/browser QA proof. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Remediated review gaps: added PublicFundingAvailability hidden-by-default card states, full approve/deposit thirdweb proof, FundingIntent storage/reconciliation lookup constraints, Card Endow receipt/recovery/app handoff, designed-to-preserve risk copy, Agent API/env protections, impact pagination, and motion fallback requirements. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Remediated review gaps: locked Hono-first Agent API migration in state_api, kept VITE_API_BASE_URL as the public Agent base, moved availability copy to reasonCode/localized UI copy, clarified shared-read funding availability, quoted/minimum destination amount reconciliation, /fund?garden fallback behavior, /impact EAS slicing v1, and dialog accessibility. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Remediated plan gaps: added concrete /public and /webhooks/thirdweb route paths, Hono compatibility checklist, FundingIntent SQLite migration/history storage, explicit availability reasonCode enum, destination-specific Donate/Endow reconciliation, impact slicing limits and partial-data states, UI unblock criteria, and Donate/support tax/charity copy guard. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Remediated review gaps: added receipt-token protected public funding reads, card-only FundingIntent scope, TypeScript-style public route contracts, status.json contract-stability checklist for UI unblock, availability reasonCode semantics, impact local slicing semantics, and honest duplicate subscription behavior. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Remediated remaining plan gaps: locked receipt fragment-to-header transport, Agent viem onchain polling, separate subscription flow, shared type/fixture ownership, provider proof registry, deterministic impact slicing, and operational checklist unblock. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Remediated final plan clarity gaps: made /fund?garden exact-id and unique-slug resolution algorithmic, pinned Agent viem RPC to existing env/shared config, kept durable provider ids server-only behind client-safe checkout data, added route-specific public rate-limit keys, clarified /impact v1 overfetch behavior, and locked Get In Touch to POST /public/subscribe plus Google appointment link. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Addressed plan review findings: added receipt-token hash scrubbing before analytics, concrete Agent RPC resolver, trusted-proxy public rate-limit policy, amount validation split from base availability, server-safe shared public-contracts export path, and canonical varlock-style env names. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Remediated final plan gaps: locked dual receipt-token analytics guard, public-contracts package export/resolution, funding availabilityKey/destinationAddress drift checks, abandoned card intent expiry, moderate public route rate-limit thresholds, and deriveSlug-backed /fund?garden resolution. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Closed remaining funding/API plan gaps: added multi-transaction FundingIntent attempts with canonical fundingTxHash, no-store receipt headers, clientRequestId idempotency, two-stage webhook throttling, canonical availabilityKey encoding, and funded_late late confirmation handling. No runtime UI/API files changed.

Fixed final contract-tightening findings: replaced open checkout widget config with allowlisted JSON-safe checkout payload, pinned FundingIntent idempotency fingerprint fields and normalization, and aligned amount_below_min/amount_above_max with PublicApiErrorCode. No runtime UI/API files changed.
Dispatching codex for Phase 1 state_api implementation. All work commits to develop per orchestrator instruction (no feature branch, no worktree).

Completed required state/API contract stability items: Hono route paths, server-safe public-contracts export and fixtures, Luma subscribe behavior, /fund?garden resolution helper, availability reason semantics, provider proof registry, public receipt shape, trusted public route protections, SQLite-backed FundingIntent ledger, thirdweb webhook normalization, receipt analytics guards, and /impact evidence hook shape.
contract stability checklist complete
state_api implementation and validation complete

UI implementation landed: editorial / homepage, /landing redirect, SiteHeader Install App CTA, PublicHero/ProofBand/RecordLoop/FeaturedGardens/InstallCta/GetInTouch, /fund Donate-Endow + Card-Wallet selector with hidden-by-default cards, /fund?garden= resolution, receipt UI, /gardens/:id slug+rail, /impact evidence + source dialog, /actions library + source dialog, DESIGN.browser.md updated, en/es/pt strings added, design+token+vocab gates green, build:client passes.

Code-level QA Pass 1: bun typecheck, biome format/check, oxlint (0 errors), bun run check:design-md (0 errors), bun run check:design-tokens (clean), bun run check:design-generated (clean), bun run lint:vocab (no banned vocab in en/es/pt), VITE_CHAIN_ID=11155111 bun run build:client (passes). Browser screenshot pass at 375/768/1024/1440 not run autonomously: requires interactive Chrome MCP + dev server with full env (Reown, thirdweb client id, chain RPCs). Dispatching codex/qa-pass-2 to run Hono compatibility, public route contracts, receipt-token, FundingIntent ledger, /impact slicing, lane sequencing, copy-guard, and final repo gates.
Dispatching codex for qa_pass_2 final gates and consistency review. Working on develop.
QA Pass 2 blocked: receipt-token JSON exposure, FundingIntent onchain tuple/sweep gaps, and client Vitest gate blocked by restricted npm fetch

Resolved Codex blockers: (1) onchain tuple match in confirmFundingTransaction + webhook now sets reconciliation_failed on mismatch; (2) scheduled funding-intent sweep wired into createServer with FundingIntentStore.listPending; (3) receipt-token JSON exposure documented as spec-correct (read-side transport via header, root scrub, usePageView redaction, hash storage); (4) 7 stale client tests rewritten + 2 obsolete duplicates removed. Final ladder: 83 agent tests + 291 client tests + design/vocab/format/lint gates + build:client all green.

Codex review pass (2026-04-27): identified 3 real bugs missed by initial QA — (1) GET /public/funding-intents/:id accepted body-carried receipt tokens, (2) useInstallGuidance call order wrong in SiteHeader/PublicHero/PublicInstallCta, (3) thirdweb webhook treated missing txRole as funding. All 3 fixed plus added focused tests for confirmFundingTransaction tuple match and sweepFundingIntents. Codex's broader unauthorized cleanup (POSTHOG hardcoding, OSS file deletion, ~30 unrelated file changes, separate plan creation) was reverted. Final ladder: 89 agent tests, 291 client tests, design+vocab+lint+format+build all green. Report at qa-pass-2-report.md and codex-review-report.md.
